posts.author: LS0107 (L)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
I need to set dynamic dropdown column that will depend on other column. As an example, I’ve got Category column(dropdown: Fruits, Berries, Veggies) and Sub-category column. I want sub category dropdown options customised based on Category. If Fruit is chosen in Category column, then apple, pear and peach should be available options in Sub Category column.
Is this something available? Found a thread from 2021 that says it’s not possible, thought will check again in case anything changed in the last years.
Thanks!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! Welcome to the monday Community. Dynamic Dropdown Columns are still not available as a feature on the platform. However, it looks like two users have submitted feature requests to flag this with the monday Product team! You can check out both requests below and upvote them to continue prioritizing this feature on the product roadmap.

Dynamic Labels in a column based on another column value Feature requests
I’d like a feature allowing dynamic option in a status column based of user choice in another column. i.e. if I change the value of column 1 to “x”, options in column 2 will be “A, b, and c”. And if I change value in column 1 to “y”, then options in column 2 will become “d, e and f”. Is it something that can be developped? Thanks!
Feature Support for Dropdown Columns Feature requests
I have found dropdown columns to be way too limited. The “managed columns” is a step in the right direction but doesn’t offer any new functionality aside from using the same static dropdowns in multiple boards. Additionally, in the current state, the “delete label” is broken. First, dynamic dropdowns. Dynamically populating a list helps keep options in sync with other systems. I’d request an option to use a formula that can reference other columns on the same board or columns on linked boards, …
